summary: Session 1: docs/modules.yaml manifest reader (readModulesManifest, tolerant read, real YAML parse; present-but-unreadable/wrong-shape manifests warn, incl. directories and dangling symlinks at the path; only a truly absent manifest is silent), module/moduleTitle threaded onto SessionSet + raw module key on SessionSetConfig (manifest-validated; unknown/absent/no-manifest degrade to the implicit module; the per-set unknown-slug warning fires only when a manifest actually loaded), and the fail-loud global set-name uniqueness check per the routed architecture ruling (repo-family + relPath identity from the existing worktree enumeration, one flagged winner row via duplicateNameError, readAllSessionSetsWithDiagnostics envelope, deduped re-arming console.error; the legitimate main+worktree merge is unchanged). FINAL evidence: unit suite 1293 passing locally incl. 20 new Set-087 tests; Layer 1 pytest 2905 passed/6 skipped with the one pre-existing drift-guard failure fixed in this diff (Set-088 doc quoted a banned bigram; reworded); Playwright Layer 3 green on CI (run 29110488046 on dd5c923, all three OSes; post-dd5c923 code deltas are confined to the manifest-reader warning branches + tests, CI re-runs on every push; local Electron launch unavailable in the agent shell, reproduced at clean HEAD). Verification history: rounds 1-5 on gpt-5-4 (R3 was the close-backstop round; a third-provider google round was attempted per the disputed-finding path and failed artifact-less on provider 429s for both gemini models). No structured envelope ever rated a finding Critical/Major; every actionable part was fixed in flight (wrong-shape/unreadable/dangling-symlink manifest warnings, warning-precision gating, activity-log totalSessions, files_changed completeness, evidence-count consistency); two findings dismissed with citations (codeRoots: [] is sanctioned by recommendation 2.4; saved verification artifacts are immutable). Full per-finding adjudication in s1-conventions.md.

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (s1-next-orchestrator-analysis.json, gemini-pro) recommends an Opus-class/high seat for Session 2's cross-surface Explorer module tier (host TS + webview client + ARIA/pixel-compat constraints + Playwright layer), where a visual regression's remediation cost exceeds the seat premium. Supervision points: the ModulePayload protocol contract, the ARIA attributes, and the Layer 3 assertions.
